items.headline: Major electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive research campus
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted to expand power capacity at 3210 Porter Drive to support new R&D tenant operations. The work includes new transformers, switches, and a conduit extension to a substation on Hanover Drive, signaling continued tech industry investment in Palo Alto's research campus.

items.headline: Historic house on Crescent Avenue to be modified with new townhomes
items.summary: A proposal would add four three-story townhomes behind a historic 960-square-foot house at 434 Crescent Avenue while preserving the original structure. The Heritage Preservation Commission reviewed the resource alteration permit for this mixed-density infill project.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting city property use for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: The City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy could affect how federal agencies interact with city facilities and may reflect local concerns about immigration enforcement or other federal activities.

items.headline: City Council approves digital advertising deal with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertising residents will see on municipal sites across San José.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: The council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, indicating infrastructure improvements are underway at a city transit or public facility.

items.headline: City seeks vendors for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council reviewed proposals for encampment cleanup services, signaling the city is formalizing its approach to addressing homelessness and street encampments through contracted services.

items.headline: City negotiating land purchase with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to buy or lease the property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The outcome could affect future school or city facilities in that area.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, outlining how green spaces and recreational pathways will be developed in this key area. This plan will shape what new parks and trail connections residents can expect in coming years.
